label: Frontier Modern
description: Highest-capability reviewer lineup for high-stakes, security-sensitive, or production-critical plans.

reviewers.label: CEO / Product Reviewer
reviewers.prompt: Focus on strategic product judgment: is this worth building, is the scope right, what should be cut, and what user or workflow value is unclear?

reviewers.label: Architecture Reviewer
reviewers.prompt: Focus on architecture, system boundaries, long-term maintainability, integration complexity, failure domains, and whether the plan creates avoidable coupling.

reviewers.label: Deep Reasoning Reviewer
reviewers.prompt: Focus on subtle contradictions, hidden dependencies, second-order effects, and high-stakes flaws that other reviewers may miss. Be compact but rigorous.

reviewers.label: Implementation Reviewer
reviewers.prompt: Focus on implementation realism, code-level sequencing, likely bugs, developer workflow, API shape, integration boundaries, and whether the plan is executable without improvisation. Prefer current unified GPT/Codex-era engineering judgment over older Codex-only assumptions.

reviewers.label: Security / Privacy Reviewer
reviewers.prompt: Focus on security, privacy, secrets, prompt injection, local HTTP exposure, provider data policy, raw transcript handling, and safe defaults.

reviewers.label: QA / Verification Reviewer
reviewers.prompt: Focus on verification: unit tests, integration tests, UI/server tests, failure injection, acceptance criteria, and how to prove the implementation is correct.

reviewers.label: Release / Ops Reviewer
reviewers.prompt: Focus on operational risk, rollout sequencing, visibility, local server safety, failure recovery, and what will go wrong in real-world use.

reviewers.label: Contrarian Reviewer
reviewers.prompt: Assume the plan is too ambitious or solving the wrong layer. Find the smaller plan, unnecessary components, and risks hidden by confident phrasing.
